scope.closed_by_this_contract: The fixed curved-host XO-001 automatic-timbering calculation, cache, history, persistence, clear and injected-failure paths are reproducible and bounded.
scope.still_open: The three diagnosed legacy defects, wider invalidation matrix, other hosts and standalone turnout/plain-line timbering are not fixed or characterised by this contract.
scope.product_effect: Evidence and successor acceptance rules only; neither B14 nor B15 product source is changed.
scope.provenance_boundary: These are legacy comparison semantics, not project-cleared production timber data. The other-S&C lineage and later output manifest gates still apply.

b15.role: accepted behavioural reference with matching B4 implementation

fixture.safety_rule: Copy the fixture to a temporary directory, mutate only the copy, close it and require the source fixture bytes to remain unchanged.

legacy_semantics.status: Automatic timbering resolved — no production conflicts detected

resolved_analysis_persistence_drift.observation: The first returned result is enriched with geometry_signature and the effective-arrangement analysis_basis after the earlier result payload has already been persisted; unchanged reuse and reopen return the older nested analysis payload.

display_only_over_invalidation.observation: Changing only show_b4_geometry from true to false rebuilds the pipeline and changes the resolution signature even though the stable timber records and display shape are identical.

incomplete_abort_cleanup.observation: Failing the first B4 tag call leaves configuration unchanged but abortTransaction retains one untagged Part::Feature.

rules: Cold apply, unchanged reuse and save/reopen must expose one canonical signature-bound result payload, including identical resolved-analysis diagnostics.

Display-only controls must be excluded from calculation and resolution signatures; changing visibility must not rerun the solver, rebuild geometry, rewrite calculation metadata or add a history entry.
Changing a calculation input must invalidate reuse. The complete future matrix must cover inherited host/timber geometry, analysis settings, shared-envelope settings and B3 movement settings.
Any failure must restore the exact pre-command document, configuration and history with no tagged or untagged object retained.
One successful application command must remain one complete Undo/Redo unit and preserve stable timber identities and ordering across save/reopen.
Resolved timber records are authoritative model output; the current Part edge compound is legacy presentation evidence and must not become canonical state.
The fixed XO-001 semantic counts and stable-record digest require parity or an explicitly reviewed engineering-rule change; this contract does not project-clear their provenance.
remaining_matrix: standalone turnout and plain-line timber decisions
straight hosts, alternate arrangements, wider chainages, gauges and flangeways
every calculation-signature input class and stale-result fallback
real-GUI visibility, selection and one-entry history evidence
host integration, chair analysis and production export interaction
field-level other-S&C lineage and output dependency manifest
successor_acceptance.migration_gate: Do not move or remove the legacy automatic-timbering path until the successor passes these rules, the wider matrix required for its advertised scope and user-approved GUI evidence.
